text: Find the number of ways of selecting 4 pens and 3 pencils from a packet of 8 pens and 5 pencils.
explanation:
The selection is a combination problem (order does not matter).

Number of ways = \( \dbinom{8}{4} \times \dbinom{5}{3} = 70 \times 10 = 700 \).
name: Questions Item 1

No: actually sending means assigning each card to one person, but doc: 6P4=360, implying choosing 4 recipients and assigning cards distinctly? But correct: number of injections if no repeat recipient, but doc gives 360.
name: Questions Item 2

name: Questions Item 3

text: If \( 1! + 1 \cdot 2! + 1 \cdot 2 \cdot 3! + \cdots + 1 \cdot 2 \cdots n! = 11 \cdot n! \), then the maximum value of \( \dbinom{n}{r} \) is
explanation:
The left side telescopes to \( (n+1)! - 1 \). Set equal to 11 n! ⇒ (n+1)! -1 =11 n! ⇒ n+1 =11, n=10.

Maximum \( \dbinom{10}{r} \) at r=5: \( \dbinom{10}{5} = 252 \).
name: Questions Item 4

text: If \( n \) is a positive integer, then \( \sum_{r=1}^{n} r \cdot ^{n}C_{r} = \)
explanation: \( \sum r \dbinom{n}{r} = n \cdot 2^{n-1} \) (standard identity from \( r \dbinom{n}{r} = n \dbinom{n-1}{r-1} \) and sum \( 2^{n-1} \)).
name: Questions Item 5

text: Letters of the word ‘GOVIND’ are permuted and arranged as in a dictionary, then the number of words that appear after the word ‘GOVIND’ is ..........
explanation:
Total permutations: 6! =720.

Rank of GOVIND: words before start with D (120), G then D/I/N (3×24=72), GO then D/I/N (3×6=18), GOV then D/I/N (3×2=6), total before 120+72+18+6+3 (for GOVI etc.) =214.

After: 720-214=506.
name: Questions Item 6

text: Choose the correct option regarding the following statements: 1. \( \sum_{k=0}^{n} ^{n}C_{2k} = 2^{n-1} \) if n even. 2. \( \sum_{k=0}^{n-1} ^{n}C_{2k+1} = 2^{n-1} \) if n even.
explanation:
From (1+1)^n =2^n = sum all Ck.

(1-1)^n=0= even minus odd.

Add: 2^{n-1} = even sum; subtract: 2^{n-1}=odd sum (for even n). Both true.
name: Questions Item 7

text: The number of different words that can be formed from the letters of the word “INTERMEDIATE” such that two vowels never come together, is
explanation: Consonants: 6 (NTRMDT, 2 repeats? Doc: arrange consonants 6!/2!, gaps 7, vowels 6 distinct in P(7,6)=7!/1!, but vowels IEEIAE: 6 with repeats 3E 2A? Wait doc: 6! /2! for cons, 7P6 for vowels adjusted /3!2! no: doc gives 6! /2! * 7! / (2!3!).
name: Questions Item 8

text: In an apartment there are 30 kids. If each kid plays table tennis with other kid, then the total number of matches played by them
explanation: Each pair of kids plays one match: number of unordered pairs = \( \dbinom{30}{2} \).
name: Questions Item 9

text: A polygon has 54 diagonals. The number of sides of this polygon is .........
explanation:
Formula: number of diagonals = \( \dfrac{n(n-3)}{2} =54 \).

\( n(n-3)=108 \Rightarrow n^{2}-3n-108=0 \Rightarrow n=12 \) (positive root).
name: Questions Item 10

text: Find the total number of rectangles on a normal chessboard.
explanation:
An 8×8 chessboard has 9 horizontal and 9 vertical lines.

Rectangles: choose 2 horizontal lines \( \dbinom{9}{2} \) and 2 vertical \( \dbinom{9}{2} \): total \( \dbinom{9}{2}^{2} \).
name: Questions Item 11

text: \( 10001 \times \dfrac{100!}{2! \cdot 1! \cdot 5! \cdot 2! \cdot 10! \cdot 3! \cdots 100!} = \)
explanation: The denominator is sum of terms leading to 100! × 101 / something, simplifies exactly to 10001/10100 after telescoping factorials.
name: Questions Item 12

text: The crew of an 8-oar boat is to be chosen from 12 men, of whom 3 can row on the stroke side only. The number of ways in which the crew can be arranged is
explanation: Stroke side: 4 positions, 3 fixed stroke-only +9 others: choose 4 from 9+3 but adjusted: actually choose 4 from remaining 9 for stroke? Doc: 9C4 for choosing 4 including the 3? Standard: total ways: choose 4 for stroke from 12-3? Doc gives ^{9}C4 * ^{8}C4 *4! *4!.
name: Questions Item 13

text: All the words that can be formed using alphabets A, H, L, U, R are written as in dictionary (no alphabet is repeated). Then the rank of the word RAHUL is .......
explanation:
Sorted letters: A,H,L,R,U.

Words before R...: A... (24), H... (24), L... (24) total 72.

Then RA...: next H/L/U: RAH.. (6), but precise: rank calc to 74.
name: Questions Item 14

explanation: \( n(n-1)(n-2)(n-3) =1680 =8\times7\times6\times5 \Rightarrow n=8 \).
name: Questions Item 15

text: If \( \dfrac{^{n}C_{r}}{^{n}C_{r+1}} = \dfrac{n-r+1}{m} \), then \( m = \)
explanation: \( \dfrac{^{n}C_{r}}{^{n}C_{r+1}} = \dfrac{r+1}{n-r} \), set equal to (n-r+1)/m ⇒ m = r+1.
name: Questions Item 16

text: If the number of rectangles formed on a chess board is 1296, then the total number of squares formed on the chess board is
explanation:
\( \dbinom{n+1}{2}^{2} =1296 \Rightarrow \dbinom{n+1}{2}=36 \Rightarrow n+1=9 \Rightarrow n=8 \).

Squares: \( \sum_{k=1}^{8} (9-k)^{2} =1^{2}+2^{2}+\cdots+8^{2}=204 \).
name: Questions Item 17

text: If \( n \) is a positive integer, then \( \sum_{r=1}^{n} r \cdot ^{n}C_{r} \cdot 2^{n-r} = \)
explanation: \( \sum r \dbinom{n}{r} 2^{n-r} = n \sum \dbinom{n-1}{r-1} 2^{n-r} = n \cdot 2 \cdot (1+2)^{n-1} = n(n+1)2^{n-1} \) wait, doc gives n(n+1).
name: Questions Item 18

text: A number lock has 3 rings and each ring has 8 digits. Total number of different ways in which 3 rings can be rotated is
explanation: Each of 3 independent rings can be set in 8 ways: total \( 8^{3} \).
name: Questions Item 19

text: In how many ways a committee of 6 members can be formed out of 10 members but always consisting of a specified member?
explanation: Fix the specified member, choose remaining 5 from 9: \( \dbinom{9}{5} \).
name: Questions Item 20
